label: ExplanationCategory

label: ExplanationCategory

label: ExplanationCategory

label: ExplanationCategory

label: ExplanationCategory

description: Point to the likely code region and describe what is suspicious there.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: LocateIssueInStudentCode

description: Restate the key programming rule in plain language.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: RestateCoreRule

description: Provide a minimal counterexample showing why the student logic fails.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: CounterExample

description: Walk through one concrete execution trace or edge case.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: TraceExecutionNarration

description: Contrast the current code behavior with the corrected version.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: CompareCurrentAndFixedLogic

description: Recommend a modern safer alternative such as RAII or std::format.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: ModernPracticeRecommendation

description: Start with mild hints and become increasingly explicit only if needed.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: HintEscalation

description: Suggest next concepts or exercises for strengthening the weak area.
label: FeedbackPattern
name: RemediationPath

description: Anchor explanation to a specific line or range of lines.
label: ContextAnchorType
name: LineNumberAnchor

description: Anchor explanation to a concrete condition, expression, or statement.
label: ContextAnchorType
name: ExpressionAnchor

description: Anchor explanation to a variable whose value or lifetime is central.
label: ContextAnchorType
name: VariableAnchor

description: Anchor explanation to a failing boundary or counterexample test.
label: ContextAnchorType
name: TestCaseAnchor

description: Anchor explanation to a compiler or analyzer finding.
label: ContextAnchorType
name: AnalyzerFindingAnchor

description: Anchor explanation to a sequence of runtime states or steps.
label: ContextAnchorType
name: ExecutionTraceAnchor

description: Revisit strict versus non-strict inequalities and zero-based iteration.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewLoopBoundaryReasoning

description: Practice indexing rules and traversal over fixed and dynamic sequences.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewArrayIndexingAndTraversal

description: Review pointer validity, ownership, free/delete semantics, and lifetime boundaries.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewPointerValidityAndLifetime

description: Practice allocate-use-release reasoning and matching ownership operations.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewManualMemoryLifecycle

description: Reinforce the idea that every non-void path must produce a value.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewFunctionReturnContracts

description: Train on defining precise base cases and shrinking recursive state.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewRecursiveBaseCaseAndProgress

description: Study invalidation rules, view lifetimes, and non-owning range semantics.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewIteratorInvalidationAndRangeLifetimes

description: Study single ownership, shared ownership, moved-from states, and RAII design.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewRAIIAndOwnershipModels

description: Review virtual destructors, base pointers, and polymorphic object cleanup.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewPolymorphicDestruction

description: Study strong/basic/no-throw guarantees and exception-safe cleanup design.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewExceptionSafetyAndCleanup

description: Practice matching types to format specifiers and prefer type-safe formatting APIs.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewFormattingAndTypeSafeOutput

description: Study locking discipline, predicate waits, and shared-state synchronization.
label: RemediationPlan
name: ReviewSynchronizationAndConditionProtocols

description: Explain why a boundary that is one too small or too large breaks traversal logic.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainOffByOneBoundaryBug

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateBoundaryCondition
text: Point to the loop condition or index access that determines the last iteration.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RestateValidIndexRange
text: Explain that a sequence of length n is normally indexed from 0 to n-1.

label: ExplanationStep
name: WalkEdgeCaseAndPatch
text: Walk through the boundary test case and show the corrected strict or non-strict condition.

description: Explain why the loop never reaches its exit condition and how to restore progress.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainInfiniteLoop

label: ExplanationStep
name: FindProgressVariable
text: Identify the variable or state that should move toward loop termination.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ShowWhyExitIsNeverReached
text: Show that the condition stays true forever or moves in the wrong direction.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RepairUpdateOrCondition
text: Adjust the update or condition so each iteration moves toward termination.

description: Explain why the current index can exceed valid sequence bounds.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainArrayOutOfBounds

label: ExplanationStep
name: IdentifyUnsafeIndex
text: Point to the index expression that can become equal to or larger than the container length.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RunBoundaryCounterExample
text: Use the smallest failing boundary case to show the invalid access.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ConstrainIndexRange
text: Rewrite the logic so every access stays within valid bounds.

description: Explain the difference between index positions and the values stored at those positions.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainIndexValueConfusion

label: ExplanationStep
name: SeparateRoles
text: Separate the variable representing the position from the value stored at that position.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ShowCurrentMixUp
text: Highlight where the code uses a value as if it were an index or vice versa.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RefactorRoles
text: Rename or restructure the variables so the two roles are not confused.

description: Explain why a nullable or invalid pointer must not be dereferenced without proof of validity.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainNullDereference

label: ExplanationStep
name: ShowUnsafeDereference
text: Point to the dereference site and the missing validity guarantee.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainValidityRequirement
text: Explain that dereference is only safe when the pointer definitely refers to a live object.

label: ExplanationStep
name: GuardOrRedesignOwnership
text: Add a guard or redesign the ownership model to make invalid states impossible.

description: Explain how allocation creates ownership obligations and why missing release causes leaks.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainMemoryLeak

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateAllocationOwnership
text: Point to the allocation site and ask who is responsible for releasing it.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ShowMissingReleasePath
text: Show the path where the resource is never released or returned safely.

label: ExplanationStep
name: IntroduceLifecycleFix
text: Add the missing release or replace manual ownership with RAII/smart pointers.

description: Explain why a handle becomes invalid immediately after its resource is released.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainUseAfterFree

label: ExplanationStep
name: TraceReleaseThenUse
text: Trace the point where the resource is released and the later point where it is still used.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainEndedLifetime
text: Explain that releasing the underlying storage ends the object lifetime for that handle.

label: ExplanationStep
name: InvalidateOrTransferOwnership
text: Stop using the handle after release or replace manual lifetime management with safer ownership.

description: Explain why the same resource must be released exactly once.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainDoubleFree

label: ExplanationStep
name: FindDuplicateReleasePath
text: Locate the two paths that release the same resource.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainSingleOwnershipRule
text: Explain that one owned resource must have one final release operation.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RestructureOwnership
text: Make ownership unique or centralize release so it happens only once.

description: Explain why reading a variable before valid initialization leads to unstable behavior.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainUninitializedValueUse

label: ExplanationStep
name: FindFirstRead
text: Find the first read of the variable before any guaranteed assignment.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainInitializationRequirement
text: Explain that a variable must be initialized before its value is used.

label: ExplanationStep
name: InitializeBeforeUse
text: Introduce an explicit meaningful initialization before the first read.

description: Explain that every non-void function path must return a valid value.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainMissingReturnValue

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateMissingPath
text: Locate the control-flow path that ends without returning a value.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainContract
text: Explain the contract of a non-void function: every path must produce a result.

label: ExplanationStep
name: AddReturnToAllPaths
text: Make every branch return a valid value or redesign the function signature.

description: Explain that recursion needs a reachable stopping condition that matches the smallest solvable case.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainWrongRecursiveBaseCase

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateBaseCaseCondition
text: Highlight the stopping condition and check whether it can actually be reached.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ShowFailingInput
text: Run a small input where the recursion should stop but does not stop correctly.

label: ExplanationStep
name: DefineReachableBaseCase
text: Rewrite the stopping condition so the smallest case terminates correctly.

description: Explain why recursive calls must reduce the problem size toward the base case.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainNoRecursiveProgress

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateRecursiveArgument
text: Find the argument or state passed into the recursive call.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ShowLackOfReduction
text: Show that the recursive input does not become simpler or smaller.

label: ExplanationStep
name: MakeProgressExplicit
text: Change the recursive call so it moves toward the base case on every step.

description: Explain why some container operations invalidate iterators, references, or views.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainIteratorInvalidation

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateModificationAndUse
text: Locate the container modification and the later iterator or view use.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainInvalidationRule
text: Explain that some operations reallocate or erase elements and invalidate observers.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RefreshIteratorsOrChangePattern
text: Reacquire iterators after modification or change to a safer traversal pattern.

description: Explain the valid but unspecified state of moved-from objects and how to use them safely.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainUseAfterMove

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateMoveAndLaterRead
text: Locate the move operation and the subsequent read that assumes the old value is still there.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainMovedFromState
text: Explain that moved-from objects remain valid but their value is not the old owned resource.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ReinitializeOrAvoidDependence
text: Reinitialize the object or avoid reading semantic value after the move.

description: Explain why deleting a derived object through a base pointer requires a virtual base destructor.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainMissingVirtualDestructor

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateDeleteThroughBase
text: Locate the delete operation performed through a base-class pointer or reference-like owner.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainPolymorphicDestructionRule
text: Explain that the base destructor must be virtual for derived cleanup to run correctly.

label: ExplanationStep
name: AddVirtualDestructor
text: Declare the base destructor virtual and verify derived cleanup now happens safely.

description: Explain why a resource-owning class must define consistent special member behavior or delegate ownership to RAII types.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainRuleOfFiveViolation

label: ExplanationStep
name: IdentifyOwnedResourceClass
text: Identify the class that manually manages a resource such as dynamic memory or a handle.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainSpecialMemberConsistency
text: Explain why copy, move, destruction, and assignment must agree on ownership semantics.

label: ExplanationStep
name: PreferRAIIOrCompleteSpecialMembers
text: Prefer composing RAII types or define the needed special members consistently.

description: Explain how exceptions can bypass manual cleanup and break invariants when ownership is not exception-safe.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainExceptionSafetyViolation

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateExceptionalPath
text: Locate the operation that can throw and the cleanup that may be skipped.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainInvariantAndCleanupRisk
text: Explain what resource or invariant becomes inconsistent if an exception interrupts the path.

label: ExplanationStep
name: AdoptRAIIBoundaries
text: Move resource cleanup into RAII objects so normal and exceptional exits behave the same.

description: Explain that formatting placeholders and argument types must agree.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainFormatSpecifierMismatch

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateFormatMismatch
text: Point to the formatting call where placeholder and argument type disagree.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainTypeAgreementRule
text: Explain that formatting APIs interpret arguments according to the placeholder contract.

label: ExplanationStep
name: CorrectSpecifierOrUpgradeAPI
text: Choose the correct specifier or use a type-safe formatting API when available.

description: Explain the correct waiting protocol for condition variables and shared state synchronization.
label: ExplanationTemplate
name: ExplainConditionVariableMisuse

label: ExplanationStep
name: LocateWaitNotifyProtocol
text: Locate the shared state, the lock, and the wait/notify operations.

label: ExplanationStep
name: ExplainPredicateWaitRule
text: Explain that waiting should re-check the predicate while holding the correct lock.

label: ExplanationStep
name: RepairSynchronizationProtocol
text: Protect the shared state consistently and rewrite waiting as a predicate loop.
